title: SIM Swap Account Takeover with Rapid Profile Change and Velocity Spike
narrative: Fraudster executes SIM swap to intercept SMS-based 2FA codes, gaining account access within 2-4 hours of the swap. Immediately updates account profile: new shipping address (dist1 jumps from <50 to 800+ miles), email domain changes from established provider to disposable (P_emaildomain shifts from gmail.com to tempmail variants), and phone number replaced. Within 15-45 minutes of profile modification, initiates 3-6 card-not-present transactions totaling $1,200-$3,500 targeting high-liquidity ProductCD='W' (digital goods) or 'C' (electronics). Card1_txn_count_1day spikes from baseline 0-2 to 5-8 transactions. All purchases occur during is_night=1 hours (txn_hour between 1-5 AM victim local time). Legitimate cardholder's historical card1_amt_zscore averages 0.3-0.8; post-takeover transactions show z-scores of 2.5-4.2. AVS and CVV match perfectly since fraudster accessed saved payment methods.
indicators: dist1 increases by 750+ miles within 1 hour of account login from new device
P_emaildomain changes from established provider to disposable/temporary email service
card1_txn_count_1day jumps from 0-2 baseline to 5-8 transactions within 45 minutes
3-6 transactions totaling $1,200-$3,500 with ProductCD='W' or 'C' within 1 hour of profile change
All transactions occur during txn_hour 1-5 AM in cardholder's historical timezone
card1_amt_zscore spikes from historical 0.3-0.8 to 2.5-4.2 across transaction burst
Phone number modification timestamp precedes transaction burst by 10-40 minutes
summary: Account accessed from new device shows immediate profile changes—shipping address jumps 750+ miles, email switches to disposable domain, phone number updated—followed within minutes by a burst of 5-8 high-value transactions ($1,200-$3,500) for digital goods or electronics, all occurring between 1-5 AM in the victim's timezone. Transaction amounts show z-scores of 2.5-4.2 versus the cardholder's normal 0.3-0.8 baseline, despite perfect AVS/CVV matches.
reasoning: The fraudster has hijacked the victim's phone number via SIM swap to intercept 2FA codes, granting full account access to saved payment methods and personal data. Rapid profile updates secure a fraudulent delivery address while the nighttime transaction velocity and amount spike indicate urgency to monetize the takeover before the legitimate owner wakes up and notices unauthorized activity.
indicator_explanations: Account location jumps hundreds of miles within an hour of suspicious login
Email switches from legitimate provider to throwaway temporary service
Transaction frequency explodes from near-zero to aggressive burst pattern
High-value digital goods or cash equivalents purchased immediately after profile tampering
All fraudulent activity occurs during cardholder's typical sleeping hours
Purchase amounts suddenly far exceed this card's normal spending behavior
Phone number was changed shortly before the spending spree began
